mechanism_narrative: Rising real money supply (RSAFS) signals liquidity expansion that lowers equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window despite rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers earnings recession expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of triggered months despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals broad demand contraction, forcing equity repricing and generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a pause in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals policy normalization, triggering short-term equity rallies over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of tightening-cycle pauses.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding corporate output capacity and revenue potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window despite positive INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) accelerate beyond 1.5 percent year-over-year, consumer spending power expands, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 65 percent of high-growth payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real money supply (RSAFS) contracts while the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) accelerates indicates stagflationary pressure, compressing profit margins and causing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns exceed negative returns by greater than 20 percent during divergence periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) breaches 205, inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.
